[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2025-04-11
[TITLE]: A Red Fox Den Goes Live—For the First Time in 7 Years! – PixCams, Inc.
[METADATA/INTRO]: A Red Fox Den Goes Live—For the First Time in 7 Years! WildWatch Weekly A Red Fox Den Goes Live—For the First Time in 7 Years! Live Cams Latest Posts Store Facebook YouTube One of the six kits at the...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Despite their bold appearance, red foxes are remarkably secretive, especially during the denning season. They dig or repurpose burrows—often called dens—to raise their young in spring. These dens are usually well-hidden, often located in wooded areas, under brush piles, or along slopes with good drainage. A typical litter contains 4–6 kits, and the vixen will care for them tirelessly for several weeks while the male (if present) provides food. 
 Red foxes are most active during the early morning and evening hours (crepuscular), though they may venture out at night as well. While they generally live alone or in small family groups, foxes communicate through a variety of vocalizations, tail movements, and scent marking. Their haunting screams and barks are often heard during the breeding season in late winter.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2023-03-20
[TITLE]: Animal control: leave foxes near your home alone this denning season
[METADATA/INTRO]: Animal control: leave foxes near your home alone this denning season ‘They do not want to hunt and eat your children, mate with your dog, or kill your cat.’...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Coyotes are notorious for finding fox dens, digging out the kits, and killing them. 
 This is why a mother fox will make her den where coyotes generally won’t go, such as under a porch, shed, garage, barn, or side of a hill to try to keep her little family safe. 
 “Please offer them a short-term rental because this is not a permanent situation,” McCleary said. “If you are lucky enough to see how beautiful an adult fox is, or witness the kits playing (at a distance of course), you will be glad you did!” 
 You may not see red foxes for long since they tend to to change dens several times during the season.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2020-01-26
[TITLE]: Fact Check: Truth behind mother fox feeding koalas during Australia bushfires
[METADATA/INTRO]: Fact Check: Truth behind mother fox feeding koalas during Australia bushfires A video of a red fox breastfeeding has gone viral with the claim that it is feeding baby koalas, and relating it to the bushfires in Australia....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: We could not independently verify as to who shot these videos and where. But since this video is available from 2014 and other videos confirm that the mother fox is feeding her kits, the viral claim that it is related to the Australian bushfires is misleading.
